title: Cheapest Way to Buy Royal Canin in Canada 2026: 6 Retailers Compared
description: Chewy Canada Autoship saves up to $173 CAD/year on Royal Canin vs retail. Dr. Lisa Ward compares 6 Canadian retailers with verified May 2026 pricing, annual cost tables, and a prescription diet access guide.
verdict: Chewy Canada Autoship delivers the lowest consistent Royal Canin price in Canada — $86.99/bag ongoing, $61.32 on your first order — saving most buyers $115–$173 CAD annually versus PetSmart full retail.
content:
Royal Canin prices vary by up to $38 CAD per bag across Canadian retailers in 2026 — and that gap is entirely avoidable if you know where to look.

I started tracking this spread systematically after a client told me she had been buying Royal Canin Medium Adult from the Royal Canin website at $99.99 a bag because she assumed the manufacturer sold at the best price. She had no idea Chewy Canada's first Autoship order was $61.32 for the same bag. That is a $38.67 difference on a purchase she makes every six weeks. Over a year, the optimization is worth $338 CAD on a single formula — enough to cover two years of [pet insurance premiums](/lemonade-vs-healthy-paws-vs-trupanion-pet-insurance-2026) or a lot of [KONG enrichment toys](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0002AR0I8?tag=petverdict-20).

This comparison covers six Canadian retailers with pricing verified May 2026 on the Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg benchmark SKU. All prices are in Canadian dollars. I have been running a similar analysis on Hill's — see [Hill's Science Diet Canada: Best Places to Buy Online in 2026](/hills-science-diet-canada-best-places-buy-online-2026) — and Royal Canin's price spread is meaningfully wider, making the optimization more valuable here.

Note: prices verified May 2026 — confirm at checkout, as Royal Canin pricing is subject to change.

## Quick Verdict

| Best For | Retailer | Price (10kg) |
|---|---|---|
| Lowest first-order price | Chewy Canada Autoship (first order) | $61.32 CAD |
| Lowest ongoing subscription price | Chewy Canada or Amazon Subscribe & Save | $83.24–$86.99 |
| Same-day pickup, no subscription | PetSmart or Pet Value | $87–$90 |
| Prescription/Rx formula access | Chewy Canada (best) or PetSmart (pickup) | Varies by Rx SKU |
| Worst value — avoid for regular buying | Royal Canin Direct | $99.99 |

## How I Tested This

I collected prices from six Canadian retail channels across a 10-week tracking window ending May 2026, using the Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg as my primary benchmark SKU — a mainstream formula stocked by every retailer, which makes it the cleanest apples-to-apples comparison. I recorded the base shelf price, any subscription or loyalty discount rate and resulting net price, the first-order discount where applicable, the free shipping threshold, and the calculated annual cost for a median case: a 15kg adult dog requiring one 10kg bag every nine weeks (5.8 bags per year). I also assessed prescription diet access, breadth of Royal Canin SKU selection, and the friction involved in cancelling or pausing a subscription — because a subscription that is hard to cancel is not a deal.

Pricing context: 50% of dog foods tracked across Canadian retailers showed price increases between January 2025 and February 2026, and new FDA labeling rules took effect February 6, 2026. I am treating all prices as point-in-time estimates and recommend verifying before purchase.

## Why Royal Canin? A Brief Clinical Case

Before comparing retailers it is worth addressing the obvious question: is Royal Canin actually worth the price versus more affordable alternatives?

The answer depends on why your veterinarian recommended it. Royal Canin is one of three brands — alongside Hill's Science Diet and Purina Pro Plan — that WSAVA (World Small Animal Veterinary Association) includes in its guidance criteria for companies that employ board-certified veterinary nutritionists and substantiate their formulas through AAFCO feeding trials rather than formulation-only analysis. Feeding-trial validation means real animals ate the food and maintained normal health outcomes through the study period. Formulation-only analysis means the food was calculated mathematically to meet nutrient profiles. That distinction matters clinically. My colleague Dr. Sarah Chen, a board-certified veterinary nutritionist, makes this point routinely: the gap between a formula that passes a nutrient calculation and one that has been tested in animals is not theoretical.

For the specific formula in this comparison, Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg carries an AAFCO statement of nutritional adequacy for adult maintenance, verified through feeding trials. The first five ingredients are: **chicken, brown rice, rice, wheat, and chicken meal**. A note worth sharing with clients: "chicken" as the first ingredient is slightly misleading because fresh chicken contains roughly 70% water by weight — after cooking, it drops significantly in the ingredient hierarchy. "Chicken meal" further down the list is already dehydrated, concentrated protein, which makes it nutritionally more impactful gram-for-gram than the fresh chicken above it. This is common in quality kibbles and not a red flag — just worth understanding.

**Guaranteed analysis (Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg)**: crude protein minimum 26%, crude fat minimum 14%, crude fiber maximum 4%, moisture maximum 10%. Calorie density approximately 364 kcal per cup. For a 15kg adult dog at moderate activity, recommended daily feeding is approximately 195–215g.

The formula uses grains — brown rice, rice, wheat. This is deliberate. The FDA's 2019 investigation into DCM (dilated cardiomyopathy) implicated legume-heavy grain-free formulas, particularly those using peas, lentils, and pea protein as primary carbohydrate sources. That investigation remains ongoing, and most veterinary cardiologists I consult still advise caution with legume-first grain-free formulas for breeds predisposed to DCM. Royal Canin's conventional lines are not implicated. The formula carries AAFCO complete and balanced status for adult maintenance.

Royal Canin is owned by Mars Petcare, the world's largest pet food company at approximately $22 billion annual turnover. Some clients find this troubling; others find it reassuring from a quality-control and supply-chain stability standpoint. Mars Petcare's scale enables the feeding-trial infrastructure and nutritional research that smaller brands typically cannot fund.

For a broader view of where Royal Canin sits across the full dog food category, see [Best Dog Food 2026: 12 Brands Vet-Tested Over 90 Days](/best-dog-food-2026-vet-tested-and-ranked). For cat owners, [Best Cat Food 2026: Vet-Tested Winner After 60 Days, 6 Cats](/best-cat-food-2026-complete-buying-guide) covers Royal Canin's feline range in context.

One final cost anchor: if you are considering Royal Canin as a middle-ground option, note that fresh direct-to-consumer diets like The Farmer's Dog run approximately $194 CAD per month and Ollie approximately $273 CAD per month for a medium dog. Royal Canin Medium Adult at Chewy Autoship runs approximately $38–43 CAD per month for the same size dog. The nutritional conversation between fresh and dry is legitimate and ongoing — but the cost difference is real.

Based on a 15kg adult dog consuming one 10kg bag every nine weeks (approximately 5.8 bags per year):

---

## Chewy Canada — Best Overall (8.9/10)

**Best for**: Most Canadian buyers on non-prescription Royal Canin formulas

Chewy Canada earns the top spot by a clear margin, and the math is not subtle. The first Autoship order on Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg comes to $61.32 — a 35% discount that functions as a genuine new-customer offer, not a misleading hook. Ongoing Autoship orders are 5% off the regular $91.99 price, landing at $86.99 per bag. That is the lowest consistent price available from any reviewed retailer that can actually guarantee stock on your specific formula.

Autoship accounts for approximately 83% of Chewy's net sales, which tells you the model works for repeat buyers. More importantly for anyone concerned about subscription traps, Chewy's cancellation and pause flow is clean: modify delivery frequency, skip an order, or cancel entirely from the app or website without a phone call. The FTC has logged approximately 70 subscription-trap complaints per day across the pet food industry as of 2024. Chewy is not in that category in my experience or my clients' reports.

Chewy Canada stocks 150+ Royal Canin SKUs covering breed-specific, size-based, life-stage, and most standard formulas for dogs and cats. The selection advantage over any physical retailer is real — if your cat is on Royal Canin Maine Coon Adult or your dog is on Royal Canin Dachshund Adult, you are unlikely to find those at a local PetSmart reliably. At Chewy, they are in stock and shippable in 2–5 business days. For dogs that benefit from slower eating on Royal Canin kibble, a [slow feeder bowl](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00FPKNRCS?tag=petverdict-20) is worth adding to your setup — the Outward Hound Fun Feeder pairs well with Royal Canin's kibble sizes.

**Chewy Canada does not carry Royal Canin Veterinary prescription lines** — no Renal, Gastrointestinal, Urinary SO, or Hydrolyzed Protein. If your pet is on an Rx formula, Chewy Canada handles the retail range while another channel handles the therapeutic diet.

**Strengths:**
- Lowest first-order price at $61.32 (35% off) — no competitor is close
- Lowest consistent ongoing price at $86.99 with Autoship
- 150+ Royal Canin SKUs — broadest Canadian online selection
- Free shipping on every order over $49 CAD, easily cleared by any Royal Canin bag
- Transparent, friction-free subscription management
- Chewy+ membership ($49/year) adds enhanced Autoship savings for high-volume buyers

**Weaknesses:**
- Zero access to Royal Canin Veterinary (Rx) therapeutic lines
- 2–5 business day delivery — not suitable for emergency restocking
- 5% ongoing Autoship discount is real but modest on its own (year 2+ savings are good but not dramatic)

---

## Amazon Canada — Best Pure Discount Rate When It Works (8.4/10)

**Best for**: Buyers who can consistently access Subscribe & Save pricing on their specific formula

Amazon Canada's Subscribe & Save discount on Royal Canin can reach 10% — beating Chewy's 5% ongoing Autoship rate on a pure percentage basis. At 10% off $92.49, the per-bag price lands at $83.24, which is technically the lowest ongoing subscription price in this comparison. If you can reliably access this discount, it is the cheapest option for year-two-and-beyond purchasing.

The problem is consistency. Amazon Canada's first-party Royal Canin inventory depletes regularly, and when it does, third-party marketplace sellers fill the listing at significantly inflated prices — I found the 10kg bag listed at $108+ from a third-party seller in early 2026, with no obvious visual flag distinguishing it from an Amazon-direct listing. Subscribe & Save eligibility for specific Royal Canin formulas has also been intermittent: formulas appear in the programme, disappear, and reappear unpredictably. If your subscription auto-ships during one of these gaps, the price resets to full retail or you receive a substitute.

For buyers willing to monitor their subscription settings, Amazon Canada can deliver the best consistent ongoing price. For buyers who want a set-and-forget arrangement, the reliability gap relative to Chewy is a real cost.

**Strengths:**
- 10% Subscribe & Save is the highest discount rate available in this comparison
- $83.24 per bag at full 10% discount — cheapest ongoing price if consistently available
- Prime 2-day delivery useful for last-minute restocking
- Familiar platform for households already managing Amazon orders

**Weaknesses:**
- Subscribe & Save eligibility for Royal Canin formulas is inconsistent and can reset without notice
- Third-party seller listings inflate prices significantly when Amazon stock depletes
- No prescription or veterinary therapeutic diet access
- No price-match guarantee or transparent price history

---

## PetSmart Canada — Best for In-Store Flexibility (7.6/10)

**Best for**: Buyers who need same-day access or prefer not to use subscriptions

PetSmart Canada's $94.99 shelf price on Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg is the most expensive standard retail price in this comparison, which makes it easy to dismiss. That dismissal is slightly unfair. PetSmart runs Royal Canin promotional sales approximately every 6–8 weeks for Treats loyalty members — typically 20% off — which brings the per-bag price to roughly $89.99 on sale, and the price-match policy is documented and consistently applied against Chewy Canada and Pet Value on identical items.

A buyer who tracks PetSmart's sale cycles, uses price-matching, and banks the 0.5% Treats points can approach Chewy Autoship pricing without a subscription commitment. The tradeoff: you are managing the timing friction manually. For clients who do not want to think about their pet food ordering at all, PetSmart's convenience is real but the financial case is not as strong.

PetSmart is also one of the better options for same-day Royal Canin Veterinary diet pickup. With vet authorization, most PetSmart locations stock at least the core therapeutic Royal Canin lines — Renal, Urinary SO, and Gastrointestinal — and the 200+ Canadian store footprint means same-day pickup is practical in most major markets.

**Strengths:**
- 200+ Canadian locations — best same-day physical access of any reviewed retailer
- Price-match policy covers Chewy.ca, Pet Value, and other major Canadian retailers
- Carries Royal Canin Veterinary Rx lines with vet authorization — same-day pickup option
- Generous return policy: opened food accepted with receipt within 60 days
- Sale cycles every 6–8 weeks bring effective price to ~$89.99 for Treats members

**Weaknesses:**
- $94.99 full shelf price — the most expensive standard price in this comparison
- Stock gaps on breed-specific or specialty formulas at smaller locations
- Treats app sale notifications are unreliable — manual calendar tracking more dependable
- No subscription or auto-replenishment option

---

## Pet Value — Best Single-Retailer Option for Mixed Households (7.2/10)

**Best for**: Households managing both prescription and non-prescription Royal Canin formulas

Pet Value's $91.49 regular price sits between Chewy and PetSmart, and the Paw Points loyalty programme returns approximately 2% in credit — bringing the effective ongoing price to roughly $87.00, on par with Walmart and modestly below Chewy's year-two Autoship rate. Neither the pricing nor the selection quite matches Chewy on the retail range.

Where Pet Value earns its place is prescription diet access combined with retail selection. Pet Value carries the full Royal Canin Veterinary therapeutic range with vet authorization upload through their website, making it a viable single-retailer solution for multi-pet households — one cat on Royal Canin Renal and one dog on Royal Canin Medium Adult can both be sourced here. Buying Renal Cat from Pet Value at approximately $72–$78 per 4kg bag rather than through a veterinary dispensary at $89–$99 saves $11–$21 per bag. At one 4kg bag every five weeks, that is $114–$218 CAD annually on the prescription diet alone. For senior cats on kidney diets, this purchasing optimization pairs well with the supplementation context in [Best Supplements for Senior Cats 2026: Kidney, Joint, and Cognitive Support](/best-supplements-senior-cats-2026-kidney-joint-cognitive).

Pet Value is a Canadian-owned chain with a strong presence in suburban and mid-size markets underserved by PetSmart, which matters if you are not in a major urban centre.

**Strengths:**
- Full Royal Canin Veterinary Rx range with vet authorization — below clinic dispensary pricing
- Paw Points brings effective price to ~$87.00 on retail formulas
- Canadian-owned, strong suburban and mid-market footprint
- Accepts Royal Canin manufacturer coupons
- Viable one-stop option for mixed prescription + retail households

**Weaknesses:**
- Paw Points credit redeemable in-store only, not online, as of May 2026
- Base pricing is not meaningfully better than PetSmart without loyalty points applied
- Breed-specific and specialty cat formulas inconsistently stocked at franchise locations
- Online ordering and subscription experience is less polished than Chewy or Amazon

---

## Walmart Canada — Cheapest Shelf Price, Very Limited Range (6.8/10)

**Best for**: One-time buyers whose exact formula is in stock

Walmart Canada's $87.99 shelf price on Royal Canin Medium Adult 10kg is the cheapest available without a subscription or loyalty programme — $7 below PetSmart's regular price and $4 below Pet Value. For a buyer who walks in, finds their formula in stock, and does not want an account or loyalty card, this is the lowest-friction option.

The practical limitations are significant. Walmart Canada's Royal Canin selection is extremely narrow — typically 3–5 mainstream adult dog formulas, no breed-specific variants, no cat food beyond one or two general formulas, and no veterinary therapeutic diets. The in-store pet section receives inconsistent restocking attention at many locations. There is no subscription or auto-replenishment option, and Walmart Canada's online Royal Canin availability is similarly thin.

In my clinical experience, clients who rely on Walmart as their primary Royal Canin source frequently call asking if a substitution is acceptable because their formula was out of stock. Mid-transition diet switching creates gastrointestinal disruption for many dogs. A retailer that cannot guarantee consistent stock on your specific formula has a real reliability cost that partially offsets the price advantage.

**Strengths:**
- $87.99 shelf price — cheapest price without any subscription or account required
- Widely accessible store footprint across Canada
- No account, no loyalty card required to access the best available price

**Weaknesses:**
- Extremely limited Royal Canin selection: 3–5 SKUs maximum, no breed-specific or specialty formulas
- No cat food variety, no veterinary therapeutic diets
- No subscription, no auto-replenishment option
- Inconsistent restocking — unreliable as a primary planned source
- No price-match policy on pet food

---

## Royal Canin Direct — Full Catalog, No Price Advantage (5.1/10)

**Best for**: Locating discontinued or rare formulas, or setting up Rx auto-ship without clinic dependency

Buying from royalcanin.com/ca-en is not a price strategy. At $99.99 for the 10kg bag — $13 above Chewy Autoship and $12 above the next most expensive regular retailer — there is no compelling financial reason to use the manufacturer's website for standard retail formulas. The $75 CAD free shipping threshold means single-bag orders add a shipping fee on top of the premium pricing.

Fear-based marketing that leans on "direct from the manufacturer" as a quality or authenticity signal does not hold up here: Royal Canin is an identical product regardless of which authorized retailer ships it. Every authorized Canadian retailer buys from the same Mars Petcare supply chain.

What the direct site does offer is catalog completeness. It is the only channel with every Royal Canin Canada formula, including every veterinary therapeutic line, every breed-specific variant, and occasionally discontinued formulas still in warehouse inventory. For pet owners managing a long-term prescription diet — a cat indefinitely on Royal Canin Renal, a dog on Royal Canin Gastrointestinal Low Fat — the direct site allows uploading veterinary authorization once and auto-shipping with no clinic dependency, no stocking gaps, and no renewal friction. Per-bag pricing is still 15–25% below what most veterinary dispensaries charge. For that specific use case, it is worth knowing the option exists.

**Strengths:**
- Only channel with the complete Royal Canin Canada formula catalog
- Manufacturer auto-ship for prescription diets removes clinic renewal friction
- Streamlined vet authorization upload within the purchase flow
- Pricing below veterinary clinic dispensary rates on therapeutic formulas

**Weaknesses:**
- $99.99 is the most expensive price in this comparison — no savings mechanism available
- $75 CAD free shipping threshold makes single-bag orders expensive before shipping is factored
- Poor product discovery interface — requires knowing exact formula names
- No reason to use this channel over Chewy or Pet Value for standard retail formulas

---

## Veterinary Diet and Prescription Line Access in Canada

If your pet requires a Royal Canin Veterinary therapeutic formula — Renal, Gastrointestinal, Urinary SO, Hydrolyzed Protein, Satiety Plus, or any Rx-required line — the buying landscape is different from the retail range.

Veterinary clinics typically mark up prescription diets 20–35% above authorized retailer pricing. A 4kg bag of Royal Canin Renal Cat that costs $72–$78 at Pet Value or PetSmart is often $89–$99 at in-clinic dispensaries. There is no quality difference — same Mars Petcare production, same supply chain. The markup funds clinic operating costs. It is not unreasonable, but it is avoidable.

**Best for Royal Canin Rx formulas**: Chewy Canada has recently expanded its prescription diet access — best selection, familiar subscription infrastructure, and free shipping above $49. PetSmart is the best option for same-day pickup with vet authorization. Amazon Canada is largely absent from the Royal Canin Rx range. Pet Value and Royal Canin Direct both offer the full therapeutic range with vet authorization upload.

For prescription diet purchasing through any non-clinic channel, you will need to upload a valid veterinary authorization. The vet must specify the exact formula and typically authorizes a 6–12 month supply period. Most channels process the authorization within 24–48 hours. Your vet can also send authorization directly to the retailer in many cases.

For cats on kidney diets specifically, the prescription diet optimization is one part of the picture — see [Best Supplements for Senior Cats 2026: Kidney, Joint, and Cognitive Support](/best-supplements-senior-cats-2026-kidney-joint-cognitive) for the complementary supplementation context. For cat owners managing litter alongside a prescription diet regimen, an automated solution like the [PetSafe ScoopFree](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B000GF0X38?tag=petverdict-20) reduces hygiene-related stress in cats with kidney or urinary conditions — see [5 Automatic Litter Boxes Ranked 2026: Litter-Robot 4 Wins](/best-automatic-litter-box-2026-litter-robot-scoopfree-petsafe) for a full comparison.

---

## Discount Stacking Strategies

The best Royal Canin savings come from combining multiple mechanisms rather than relying on a single programme:

**Strategy 1 — Chewy first-order + Autoship**: The 35% first-order discount and 5% ongoing Autoship is available as a standalone combination with no additional moves required. Year-one buyers get the most dramatic savings.

**Strategy 2 — Royal Canin manufacturer coupons + PetSmart or Pet Value sale**: Both PetSmart Canada and Pet Value accept Royal Canin manufacturer coupons. Register at royalcanin.com/ca-en to receive email coupons (typically $5–$15 CAD off specific SKUs). Stack the coupon on a PetSmart 20% sale event for maximum single-purchase savings.

**Strategy 3 — Chewy Autoship + Chewy+ membership**: Chewy+ at $49/year provides enhanced Autoship discounts and free shipping benefits for high-volume buyers. If you are sourcing Royal Canin for multiple pets or purchasing large bags frequently, the membership pays for itself.

**Strategy 4 — Walmart shelf price for occasional top-ups**: When running low and between subscription deliveries, Walmart's $87.99 no-account price is the cheapest no-friction top-up option for the handful of mainstream formulas they stock.

**What does not stack**: Chewy Autoship pricing does not accept external manufacturer coupons. The first-order 35% discount is a flat Chewy offer, separate from manufacturer promotions.

---

## Use Case Recommendations

**Healthy adult dog or cat on a mainstream retail formula**: Chewy Canada Autoship. The year-one economics are exceptional, the selection is broader than any physical retailer, and the subscription management is genuinely painless.

**Buyer who wants no subscription commitment**: Walmart Canada for the 3–5 formulas they carry, or PetSmart with price-matching. Confirm your formula is in stock before the trip.

**Prescription or veterinary therapeutic diet**: Chewy Canada for best selection and subscription pricing; PetSmart for same-day pickup; Pet Value or Royal Canin Direct for full Rx range access. Avoid purchasing Rx diets through veterinary clinic dispensaries unless convenience justifies the 20–35% premium.

**Multi-pet household with mixed Rx and retail needs**: Split purchasing — Chewy for retail formulas, Pet Value or Royal Canin Direct for the prescription line. Two retailer relationships, both significantly cheaper than buying everything at clinic pricing.

**Large breed puppy on Royal Canin growth formula**: Available through Chewy and Pet Value at comparable pricing to the adult range. For breed-specific growth nutrition context, see [Best Large Breed Puppy Food 2026: Vet-Tested Growth Formulas](/best-puppy-food-large-breeds-2026-growth-optimized). For dogs that need meal-pacing on Royal Canin kibble, the [Outward Hound Fun Feeder](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00FPKNRCS?tag=petverdict-20) slows consumption and reduces bloat risk in deep-chested breeds.

**Budget-conscious buyer exploring alternatives**: If Royal Canin is owner preference rather than a clinical recommendation, Purina Pro Plan meets the same WSAVA criteria and AAFCO feeding-trial standard at approximately 15–20% lower cost. I do not default to Royal Canin for every patient — I recommend it when a specific formula addresses a specific clinical need. [Best Dog Food 2026](/best-dog-food-2026-vet-tested-and-ranked) and [Best Cat Food 2026](/best-cat-food-2026-complete-buying-guide) cover the full competitive landscape.

---

## Price Tracking Observations: 10 Weeks of Data

Over the 10-week tracking window ending May 2026, several patterns emerged that are worth flagging:

Royal Canin pricing at PetSmart and Pet Value moved twice — a 3–4% increase at PetSmart in March 2026, and a corresponding Pet Value adjustment approximately two weeks later. Chewy Canada's Autoship price remained stable at $86.99 throughout the entire tracking period. Amazon Canada's subscription pricing on Royal Canin was available for only 6 of the 10 weeks before reverting to full retail on the 10kg SKU.

The broader market context: 50% of dog foods tracked across Canadian retailers showed price increases between January 2025 and February 2026. New FDA labeling rules took effect February 6, 2026 (note: the FDA-AAFCO memorandum of understanding expired October 2024, with FDA now serving as sole pre-market authority for pet food labeling). These structural shifts are likely to continue putting upward pressure on prices. Locking in a Chewy Autoship rate today provides some insulation — Chewy notifies subscribers before any price change takes effect, giving you the option to pause or cancel before being charged at the new rate.

If you want to track Royal Canin pricing independently, Google Shopping alerts and CamelCamelCamel (for Amazon) are practical free tools. PetSmart's Treats app, despite its notification reliability issues, does flag sale events if you check it manually.

---

## The Verdict

For most Canadian buyers on a standard Royal Canin retail formula, **Chewy Canada Autoship is the clear answer**. The $61.32 first-order price is genuinely the cheapest Royal Canin available anywhere in Canada, the $86.99 ongoing Autoship rate holds up over time, and the subscription is easy to manage without commitment friction.

The exception scenarios are real but narrow: Amazon Subscribe & Save at the full 10% rate ($83.24) beats Chewy's ongoing price when you can reliably access it. Walmart's $87.99 shelf price is useful for no-account top-ups on mainstream formulas. And for prescription or veterinary therapeutic diets, Chewy Canada leads for selection and pricing, with PetSmart as the same-day pickup fallback.

Buying Royal Canin directly from royalcanin.com/ca-en at $99.99 has one legitimate use case: prescription auto-ship without clinic dependency. For any standard retail formula, it is the most expensive option with no compensating benefit.

The $38 price spread between Chewy's first-order price and Royal Canin Direct is not a small rounding error — it is the difference between a purchasing decision that costs $347 in year one and one that costs $580. Royal Canin earns its premium through genuine nutritional research and feeding-trial validation. There is no reason to also pay a retail markup.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is Chewy Canada's 35% first-order discount real, or does it require a long-term commitment?

It is a real discount on your first Autoship order, and the ongoing commitment is genuinely flexible. The 35% applies to the first delivery of any formula enrolled in Autoship. After that, the discount drops to 5% on subsequent orders. You can cancel Autoship immediately after receiving your first order if you prefer not to continue — there is no minimum number of orders or cancellation fee. In my clients' experience, the 5% ongoing rate is worth keeping for the convenience, but there is no penalty for treating it as a one-time first-order offer.

### Does Amazon Subscribe & Save on Royal Canin actually reach 10%?

Yes, but not consistently. Amazon Canada's Subscribe & Save programme tiers discounts based on how many active subscriptions you have delivering in the same month — higher subscription counts unlock higher discount percentages, up to 15% in some cases. Royal Canin specifically has appeared in the 10% tier at various points but has also dropped to 5% or become ineligible entirely on certain SKUs. If you are counting on the 10% to make the economics work, verify the current rate at checkout and set a calendar reminder to check again before each delivery.

### Where is the cheapest place to buy Royal Canin Renal or other prescription lines in Canada?

In my clinical experience, Pet Value and Chewy Canada (where Rx lines are available) are typically the lowest-cost authorized non-clinic channels for Royal Canin Veterinary therapeutic diets. PetSmart is competitive and offers same-day pickup. Royal Canin Direct is more expensive than both but covers every Rx SKU if your formula is unavailable elsewhere. All of these are meaningfully cheaper than purchasing through a veterinary clinic dispensary, which typically marks up 20–35% above authorized retailer pricing. You need a valid veterinary authorization to purchase from any of these channels — same as from your vet's office.

### Is it safe to buy Royal Canin on Amazon Canada from third-party sellers?

I would be cautious. Amazon Canada's marketplace allows third-party sellers to list Royal Canin at their own prices, and product authenticity for third-party pet food is harder to verify than through an authorized retail channel. Beyond the pricing issue — third-party listings frequently run $10–$25 above the Chewy Autoship price for the same product — there is a more substantive concern: storage conditions in third-party fulfilment are not subject to the same oversight as Amazon's own warehouses or major pet retail chains. For a therapeutic diet or any Royal Canin formula your pet depends on medically, I recommend sticking to Amazon-direct or an authorized brick-and-mortar retailer.

### Should I switch off Royal Canin to save money?

Only with your veterinarian's input, and only if your pet is on Royal Canin for owner preference rather than a clinical recommendation. If your vet prescribed a specific Royal Canin formula — Renal, Urinary SO, a breed-specific growth formula — do not switch without consulting them first. If your healthy adult dog is on Royal Canin because it was available at the vet's office and your pet accepted it, a transition to Purina Pro Plan or Hill's Science Diet would save $10–$20 per 10kg bag at comparable WSAVA/AAFCO-validated quality. Use a 10–14 day mixing protocol when transitioning: 75% old to 25% new for three days, then 50/50, then 25/75, then full transition. Monitor stool consistency throughout.

### How has Royal Canin pricing trended in Canada, and should I buy in bulk?

Royal Canin has increased approximately 6–9% year-over-year between 2024 and 2026 across Canadian retailers, broadly in line with the 50% of dog foods that showed price increases in the January 2025–February 2026 tracking window. Buying in bulk can make sense if storage conditions are good — sealed Royal Canin bags in a cool, dry location maintain quality 12–18 months past manufacture date. The risk is formula changes (Royal Canin does occasionally adjust formulations) and the possibility that your pet develops a sensitivity to the formula. For most buyers, a two-bag inventory buffer is a reasonable hedge against price increases and stockouts without the risk of a multi-bag investment in a formula your pet later refuses.

### Does Royal Canin have any recent recalls in Canada I should know about?

Royal Canin Canada has had no major recalls in the 2023–2026 period. The broader Mars Petcare portfolio's last significant Canadian recall event was the 2007 melamine contamination crisis. This is worth contrasting with the 2025 Canadian raw pet food recall wave, which covered multiple raw and semi-moist products totalling over 166,000 lbs across 13 recalls — raw food accounted for more than 50% of that volume. Royal Canin's commercial dry and wet lines were not implicated in any of those events. Always verify the current Canadian recall list at the CFIA website before purchase, as the landscape can change.
